answer.content:
Socket Programming Interactive Simulator

Purpose: Explore TCP/UDP socket behavior, state transitions and packet flows. Interface is intentionally plain. Create hosts, sockets, craft packets, and control a simulated network (latency, loss, reorder). Many controls and workflows are provided.

Create Hosts & Sockets

For UDP, "Connect" sets default peer but still sends datagrams.

Manual Packet Crafting (Inject)




Network Controls






Scripting / Sequencer

Event Log

Packet Queue Viewer

Selected Socket Inspector



Connection Backlog & Pending

Utilities

messages.content:
Generate a single, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that demonstrates the concept Socket Programming with MAXIMUM interactivity and rich user control. Focus on:

- Multiple interactive controls (buttons, sliders, input fields)
- Complex state transitions and user-driven workflows
- Deep exploration capabilities with many possible interaction paths
- Rich functionality as the PRIMARY goal

IMPORTANT: Keep visual design EXTREMELY MINIMAL and BASIC - use simple black text on white background, basic HTML elements with no styling, no colors, no animations. The interface should be functional but visually plain and unpolished. Prioritize interactive richness over visual appeal.

Only respond with the complete HTML file.
